records.ety: [F., fr. LL. conductus escort, conduit. See Conduct.]

defs: A pipe, canal, channel, or passage for conveying water or fluid.
quotes: All the conduits of my blood froze up.
This is the fountain of all those bitter waters, of which, through a hundred different conduits, we have drunk.

defs: A structure forming a reservoir for water.
A narrow passage for private communication.
